item.title: Feature Highlight - Skitarii Visual Design
item.description:
Warband!

Last week we revealed the new Skitarii Alpha Primus class to the world. We are so excited for these specialist warriors of the Machine-God to join the fight on Atoma, bringing their wargear and combat expertise to the fight against the heretics!

Today we are joined by Matte and Lucas, our Art Director and Associate Art Director, to chat a bit about how we visualised this new class in Darktide.

Take it away guys! 

“As a huge Warhammer fan myself, getting the opportunity to explore something from the Adeptus Mechanicus faction was very exciting”

  • Lucas

We already touched upon the fact that the entire Darktide team has been waiting for a chance to add an Adeptus Mechanicus themed class to the game for a long time, and for many of us this really is a dream come true.

The ritualistic elements of this faction, combined with their technology is something we find really interesting, and getting to pour all of our combined knowledge and ideas into this has been such a fun journey. It’s been great to collaborate together with Games Workshop to make sure we get the look and feel of these warriors just right. 

We realised early that if we are doing an Adeptus Mechanicus class, it would be represented by the Skitarii, as they make the most sense, both for the power level of the Inquisitorial Warband, but also from a good gameplay experience perspective. 

“I think this is the closest we have been to the core of Warhammer 40.000 for a class in the game” 

  • Matte

Since the Skitarii are so iconic for Warhammer 40.000, we knew that this would be an immense task to represent in the game. Not only did we want to honor the lore, but we also wanted to make sure that the class ticked all the boxes, being represented correctly and fun to play!

Skitarii headgear concept

Skitarii backpack concept

One of the most interesting (and important) parts of the Skitarii is the blend between human and machine. The Skitarii are at their core humans, but they have been augmented by bionics to better suit the battlefield conditions they are faced with. Their limbs have been upgraded to mechanical ones, and their faces are covered in different optical enhancements and other gruesome details, all to make them better warriors. We thought a lot on how to represent this, and we hope that when you get to see the coming reveals, you can see how much care and consideration went into this.

Bionic arm concept 

The bionics themselves have been very fun to work on. Since we have always hoped to bring this class to the game, we have consciously not added too much of these to the cosmetics of the other classes. We felt that if we ever get the chance to visualise the Adeptus Mechanicus as a player class, that was going to be the time to go all out on this.

And that time is finally here!

“This has been a project long vision for the team, and I was excited for us to finalise this class”

  • Matte

Another really cool thing to work on was their iconic weaponry and adding some of them to the game. It is always a challenge to take something from the tabletop game and bring it to life. The weapons are front and center in Darktide, so it’s something we take great pride in trying to figure out how to represent in the best way possible.

Phosphor Blast Pistol firing & reload concept 

Together with Games Workshop, we always try to make the weapons believable, especially the ranged ones. A lot of work goes into trying to figure out how parts move, how the reload mechanism works and how the weapon acts as you pull the trigger. Not to mention the sound and VFX of the shots.

The weapons of the Adeptus Mechanicus are works of art, and we spent a lot of time making sure that this is also shown. The blend between high-tech, ornate wood work and fine metals make them really stand out. We thought along the lines of making them feel both archaic and advanced at the same time. 

Galvanic Rifle Concept 

One good example of this is the Galvanic Rifle. We took the existing concept art, the look of the miniatures themselves and then mixed this with references of how historical weapons work and sprinkled the advanced technology of the Adeptus Mechanicus on top.  This all blends together to make the weapon you will see in the game. It’s a long process to get everything right, but a very rewarding one when it’s done.

“There is also the gameplay aspect of it, we want to make sure the weapons look and act in a way that makes sense for the player while using them”

  • Matte

Another huge task was to visualise the room where you will create your player character. It’s the inner sanctum of the old Adeptus Mechanicus facility that Hadron Omega-7-7 has gotten access to, and it is here standard Skitarii are upgraded into the Alpha Primus variant. 

Skitarii Alpha Primus character creator ideas 

There is so much detail that has gone into this. First and foremost we wanted the mood of it to be just right. In the center there is this giant machine, the main component of the upgrade process, but the room is also filled with censer smoke and candles. We really tried to nail the feel of this being both a machine and ritual chamber. 

Skitarii human parts concept

It is here you will see the full display of the gruesome nature of the Skitarii. Suspended in the middle will be a disfigured torso and head that is the core of your character, and you get to pick the mechanical limbs and other details for it. We wanted it to feel like you are creating your very own Skitarii. 

“The hood, the face and the bionic limbs are so iconic” 

  • Lucas

Much of this will then be concealed by the hood and robes of the Adeptus Mechanicus when you are done. And since you also get to pick what Forge World your Skitarii initially came from, these come in some different colors to tie it all together.

Forge World color variations (Mars, Ryza & Branx Minor)

That’s as much as we will go into today, all in all this has been a very challenging, but rewarding, process for us, and we have so much to share with you in the upcoming weeks! 

If you want to know more about the character customization, make sure to tune in next week as we will really deep dive into this. But for now, this is us signing off.


The Skitarii Alpha Primus class will be available on the 23rd of June, but you can already wishlist it now.

 

https://store.steampowered.com/app/4355570/Warhammer_40000_Darktide__Skitarii_Class/https://store.steampowered.com/app/4355580/Warhammer_40000_Darktide__Skitarii_Class_Deluxe_Edition/Thanks to Matte and Lucas for sharing their thoughts and experience on the visual design of the new class, and thanks to all of you for reading! 

Praise the Omnissiah!

See you at the Mourningstar!

- The Darktide team
item.content:encoded:
Warband!

Last week we revealed the new Skitarii Alpha Primus class to the world. We are so excited for these specialist warriors of the Machine-God to join the fight on Atoma, bringing their wargear and combat expertise to the fight against the heretics!

Today we are joined by Matte and Lucas, our Art Director and Associate Art Director, to chat a bit about how we visualised this new class in Darktide.

Take it away guys! 

“As a huge Warhammer fan myself, getting the opportunity to explore something from the Adeptus Mechanicus faction was very exciting”

  • Lucas

We already touched upon the fact that the entire Darktide team has been waiting for a chance to add an Adeptus Mechanicus themed class to the game for a long time, and for many of us this really is a dream come true.

The ritualistic elements of this faction, combined with their technology is something we find really interesting, and getting to pour all of our combined knowledge and ideas into this has been such a fun journey. It’s been great to collaborate together with Games Workshop to make sure we get the look and feel of these warriors just right. 

We realised early that if we are doing an Adeptus Mechanicus class, it would be represented by the Skitarii, as they make the most sense, both for the power level of the Inquisitorial Warband, but also from a good gameplay experience perspective. 

“I think this is the closest we have been to the core of Warhammer 40.000 for a class in the game” 

  • Matte

Since the Skitarii are so iconic for Warhammer 40.000, we knew that this would be an immense task to represent in the game. Not only did we want to honor the lore, but we also wanted to make sure that the class ticked all the boxes, being represented correctly and fun to play!

Skitarii headgear concept

Skitarii backpack concept

One of the most interesting (and important) parts of the Skitarii is the blend between human and machine. The Skitarii are at their core humans, but they have been augmented by bionics to better suit the battlefield conditions they are faced with. Their limbs have been upgraded to mechanical ones, and their faces are covered in different optical enhancements and other gruesome details, all to make them better warriors. We thought a lot on how to represent this, and we hope that when you get to see the coming reveals, you can see how much care and consideration went into this.

Bionic arm concept 

The bionics themselves have been very fun to work on. Since we have always hoped to bring this class to the game, we have consciously not added too much of these to the cosmetics of the other classes. We felt that if we ever get the chance to visualise the Adeptus Mechanicus as a player class, that was going to be the time to go all out on this.

And that time is finally here!

“This has been a project long vision for the team, and I was excited for us to finalise this class”

  • Matte

Another really cool thing to work on was their iconic weaponry and adding some of them to the game. It is always a challenge to take something from the tabletop game and bring it to life. The weapons are front and center in Darktide, so it’s something we take great pride in trying to figure out how to represent in the best way possible.

Phosphor Blast Pistol firing & reload concept 

Together with Games Workshop, we always try to make the weapons believable, especially the ranged ones. A lot of work goes into trying to figure out how parts move, how the reload mechanism works and how the weapon acts as you pull the trigger. Not to mention the sound and VFX of the shots.

The weapons of the Adeptus Mechanicus are works of art, and we spent a lot of time making sure that this is also shown. The blend between high-tech, ornate wood work and fine metals make them really stand out. We thought along the lines of making them feel both archaic and advanced at the same time. 

Galvanic Rifle Concept 

One good example of this is the Galvanic Rifle. We took the existing concept art, the look of the miniatures themselves and then mixed this with references of how historical weapons work and sprinkled the advanced technology of the Adeptus Mechanicus on top.  This all blends together to make the weapon you will see in the game. It’s a long process to get everything right, but a very rewarding one when it’s done.

“There is also the gameplay aspect of it, we want to make sure the weapons look and act in a way that makes sense for the player while using them”

  • Matte

Another huge task was to visualise the room where you will create your player character. It’s the inner sanctum of the old Adeptus Mechanicus facility that Hadron Omega-7-7 has gotten access to, and it is here standard Skitarii are upgraded into the Alpha Primus variant. 

Skitarii Alpha Primus character creator ideas 

There is so much detail that has gone into this. First and foremost we wanted the mood of it to be just right. In the center there is this giant machine, the main component of the upgrade process, but the room is also filled with censer smoke and candles. We really tried to nail the feel of this being both a machine and ritual chamber. 

Skitarii human parts concept

It is here you will see the full display of the gruesome nature of the Skitarii. Suspended in the middle will be a disfigured torso and head that is the core of your character, and you get to pick the mechanical limbs and other details for it. We wanted it to feel like you are creating your very own Skitarii. 

“The hood, the face and the bionic limbs are so iconic” 

  • Lucas

Much of this will then be concealed by the hood and robes of the Adeptus Mechanicus when you are done. And since you also get to pick what Forge World your Skitarii initially came from, these come in some different colors to tie it all together.

Forge World color variations (Mars, Ryza & Branx Minor)

That’s as much as we will go into today, all in all this has been a very challenging, but rewarding, process for us, and we have so much to share with you in the upcoming weeks! 

If you want to know more about the character customization, make sure to tune in next week as we will really deep dive into this. But for now, this is us signing off.


The Skitarii Alpha Primus class will be available on the 23rd of June, but you can already wishlist it now.

 

https://store.steampowered.com/app/4355570/Warhammer_40000_Darktide__Skitarii_Class/https://store.steampowered.com/app/4355580/Warhammer_40000_Darktide__Skitarii_Class_Deluxe_Edition/Thanks to Matte and Lucas for sharing their thoughts and experience on the visual design of the new class, and thanks to all of you for reading! 

Praise the Omnissiah!

See you at the Mourningstar!

- The Darktide team

item.title: May Community Update
item.description:
Hi Space Marines,

May’s all about Warhammer Skulls and therefore we’ve launched Patch 13 - Purgation Update a week ago! Have you tried it already?

In this month’s Community Update, we shall reveal some Year 3 spoilers, recap Purgation Update’s content, and remind you of the ongoing Twitch Drops.

TEASING YEAR 3 👀

Lots of exciting things are coming in Year 3, and today we’re too excited to keep them under wraps, so here are a few spoilers!

Throughout Year 3, the game will receive five major updates (Patches 15 to 19). Once again, there’ll be plenty of free content for everyone, including new weapons, missions, and more. On top of that, there’ll be a Season Pass packed with Chapter cosmetics and Champion skins. 

We’ll also continue placing a strong focus on improving the user experience and fixing bugs throughout Year 3, making the game smoother, more stable, and more enjoyable for everyone. 

Now for our first spoiler: a new Class is on the way, planned for the first half of 2027! We can’t reveal which one just yet, but we can say it’s been highly requested by the community. As we did with the Techmarine, this new Class will receive its own  special Champion Skin after its release. 

Place your bets! Which Class do you think it will be? 🤔😁

The Year 3 roadmap will be revealed alongside the Second Anniversary Update. Stay tuned!

PATCH 13 - PURGATION UPDATE

Iron Hands Chapter Pack

The Iron Hands Chapter pack includes a Techmarine Champion and a skin for its signature weapon, the Omnissian Axe.

Armour pieces were added as part of the Chapter Customisation Pack: 

  • L&R Greaves

  • L&R Gauntlets

  • L&R Pauldrons & Decals

[carousel][/carousel]
Successor Chapters

Four Successor Chapters were added alongside the Iron Hands Chapter Pack, including:

  • Brazen Claws

  • Iron Lords

  • Sons of Medusa

  • Red Talons

New Operation - PURGATION

Investigate Chaos activity near a Cadian base and stop an unholy ritual. 

As you make your way across the swamps, stay alert – Chaos corruption status effect will apply if you spend too much time in the waters. 

The water corruption grows over time, and eventually, geysers may erupt near your squad, dealing damage unless you evade them.

[carousel][/carousel]
Siege Mode Update

Several new features were implemented in Siege Mode:

  • Now, you can earn weekly Accolades for completing Siege Mode. 

  • New items have been added to the shop, including a Commissar to boost Cadians, explosive mines, Tarantula Sentries and a Tech-priest to defend as he gets you currency boosts. 

  • Blessings and Atonements were introduced. It’s up to you to pick and choose them and customise your mission. Pick 1 Blessing amongst 3 every 5 waves. One Atonement is applied every 5 waves as well. You can reroll it in exchange for some Siege currency. 

  • The Hive Tyrant and Mutalith Vortex Beast can now be encountered as Bosses. And the timer got a rework too. Now, when the timer’s over, you won’t get a Game Over, but enemies will become subsequently harder to beat.

Gameplay features

For further details on the new gameplay features, we strongly recommend checking the 13.0 Patch Notes right here. 

Here’s a compendium of what’s new:

  • Battle-Barge Battle Simulator

  • Perks Loadout

  • New Ordeals

Regarding the new Ordeals, our chapter Chaplain has learnt of a number of “creative” practices with the private lobbies to quickly get the rewards, and has instructed all units to heed the following warning. We’re not going to fix these, but you’ve been warned, Space Marines!

  • Wargear Sharing

  • New Rewards in the Refinement & Redemption shop (3 Weapon Variants and 6 Armour Variants)

  • New weapon: Bolt Carbine Sidearm (One-handed)

[carousel][/carousel]

13.1 Hotfix

Earlier this week, we released a hotfix to improve the connectivity issues that occurred with the Patch 13. It also lowers the requirements to unlock the Wargear Sharing items. You can read the patch notes here for further details.

TWITCH DROPS 🎥

To celebrate the release of the Purgation Update, we’re hosting a Twitch Drops campaign until June 11 with these special rewards.

Watch any streamer on Twitch playinghttps://x.com/hashtag/SpaceMarine2?src=hashtag_clickSpace Marine 2 for 30 min, 1 and 2 hours to earn the Mark X Skull Breastplate, 100 Accolades and the brand new Victrix Honour Guard Gauntlet. 

That’s it for this month!

Thank you for supporting us and helping us improve Space Marine 2. We remain very attentive to your feedback. As a token of our appreciation, here’s a little code to unlock 150 Requisition points: GEGV-5QJJ-5P68. 

You’ll have to claim it through PROS, you can read this page to learn how.

We’ll handle a new community event in June to earn Accolades as well.

If you’d like to keep yourself informed on the latest news, make sure you follow our socials: Instagram, Facebook, X, Threads, Bluesky. 

🪽 The Emperor Protects 

-The Saber and Focus teams
item.content:encoded:
Hi Space Marines,

May’s all about Warhammer Skulls and therefore we’ve launched Patch 13 - Purgation Update a week ago! Have you tried it already?

In this month’s Community Update, we shall reveal some Year 3 spoilers, recap Purgation Update’s content, and remind you of the ongoing Twitch Drops.

TEASING YEAR 3 👀

Lots of exciting things are coming in Year 3, and today we’re too excited to keep them under wraps, so here are a few spoilers!

Throughout Year 3, the game will receive five major updates (Patches 15 to 19). Once again, there’ll be plenty of free content for everyone, including new weapons, missions, and more. On top of that, there’ll be a Season Pass packed with Chapter cosmetics and Champion skins. 

We’ll also continue placing a strong focus on improving the user experience and fixing bugs throughout Year 3, making the game smoother, more stable, and more enjoyable for everyone. 

Now for our first spoiler: a new Class is on the way, planned for the first half of 2027! We can’t reveal which one just yet, but we can say it’s been highly requested by the community. As we did with the Techmarine, this new Class will receive its own  special Champion Skin after its release. 

Place your bets! Which Class do you think it will be? 🤔😁

The Year 3 roadmap will be revealed alongside the Second Anniversary Update. Stay tuned!

PATCH 13 - PURGATION UPDATE

Iron Hands Chapter Pack

The Iron Hands Chapter pack includes a Techmarine Champion and a skin for its signature weapon, the Omnissian Axe.

Armour pieces were added as part of the Chapter Customisation Pack: 

  • L&R Greaves

  • L&R Gauntlets

  • L&R Pauldrons & Decals

[carousel][/carousel]
Successor Chapters

Four Successor Chapters were added alongside the Iron Hands Chapter Pack, including:

  • Brazen Claws

  • Iron Lords

  • Sons of Medusa

  • Red Talons

New Operation - PURGATION

Investigate Chaos activity near a Cadian base and stop an unholy ritual. 

As you make your way across the swamps, stay alert – Chaos corruption status effect will apply if you spend too much time in the waters. 

The water corruption grows over time, and eventually, geysers may erupt near your squad, dealing damage unless you evade them.

[carousel][/carousel]
Siege Mode Update

Several new features were implemented in Siege Mode:

  • Now, you can earn weekly Accolades for completing Siege Mode. 

  • New items have been added to the shop, including a Commissar to boost Cadians, explosive mines, Tarantula Sentries and a Tech-priest to defend as he gets you currency boosts. 

  • Blessings and Atonements were introduced. It’s up to you to pick and choose them and customise your mission. Pick 1 Blessing amongst 3 every 5 waves. One Atonement is applied every 5 waves as well. You can reroll it in exchange for some Siege currency. 

  • The Hive Tyrant and Mutalith Vortex Beast can now be encountered as Bosses. And the timer got a rework too. Now, when the timer’s over, you won’t get a Game Over, but enemies will become subsequently harder to beat.

Gameplay features

For further details on the new gameplay features, we strongly recommend checking the 13.0 Patch Notes right here. 

Here’s a compendium of what’s new:

  • Battle-Barge Battle Simulator

  • Perks Loadout

  • New Ordeals

Regarding the new Ordeals, our chapter Chaplain has learnt of a number of “creative” practices with the private lobbies to quickly get the rewards, and has instructed all units to heed the following warning. We’re not going to fix these, but you’ve been warned, Space Marines!

  • Wargear Sharing

  • New Rewards in the Refinement & Redemption shop (3 Weapon Variants and 6 Armour Variants)

  • New weapon: Bolt Carbine Sidearm (One-handed)

[carousel][/carousel]

13.1 Hotfix

Earlier this week, we released a hotfix to improve the connectivity issues that occurred with the Patch 13. It also lowers the requirements to unlock the Wargear Sharing items. You can read the patch notes here for further details.

TWITCH DROPS 🎥

To celebrate the release of the Purgation Update, we’re hosting a Twitch Drops campaign until June 11 with these special rewards.

Watch any streamer on Twitch playinghttps://x.com/hashtag/SpaceMarine2?src=hashtag_clickSpace Marine 2 for 30 min, 1 and 2 hours to earn the Mark X Skull Breastplate, 100 Accolades and the brand new Victrix Honour Guard Gauntlet. 

That’s it for this month!

Thank you for supporting us and helping us improve Space Marine 2. We remain very attentive to your feedback. As a token of our appreciation, here’s a little code to unlock 150 Requisition points: GEGV-5QJJ-5P68. 

You’ll have to claim it through PROS, you can read this page to learn how.

We’ll handle a new community event in June to earn Accolades as well.

If you’d like to keep yourself informed on the latest news, make sure you follow our socials: Instagram, Facebook, X, Threads, Bluesky. 

🪽 The Emperor Protects 

-The Saber and Focus teams

item.title: Hotfix 13.1
item.description:
Hotfix 13.1

GAMEPLAY AND BALANCING TWEAKS

Firearms in PvE

Gunstrike damage values restored to pre 13.0 values.

Plasma pistol variants gun strike damage adjusted to better reflect new versions speciality (i.e. damage version deals more gunstrike damage).

Siege Mode

Atonements:

  • Players will start receiving atonements starting from wave 11 on normal difficulty.

  • Players will start receiving atonements starting from wave 6 on hard difficulty.

A new indicator has been added to alert you when a new blessing becomes available.

Additional Blessing: Surplus - You can carry twice as much Equipment. Equipment spawns are doubled.

Additional Atonement: Hardened Skins - Enemies are not staggered by the first 3 hits. Damage is still dealt.

Additional Atonement: Posthumous Proliferation - Slain enemies spawn Spores or Chaos Skulls (depending on faction).

Additional Atonement: Booby Trap - Majoris-level and higher enemies explode upon death and deal Damage to Squad Members.

Ordeals

Reduced the requirements for the 6 "complete missions on absolute difficulty" class-specific ordeals from 10/30/100 to 10/25/50.

GENERAL FIXES

Crash fixes.

Consoles: Crashes when the French and Spanish VO were selected have been fixed.

Fixed a bug where several perks would reset their cooldown after switching loadouts.

Fixed a bug where several perks would stop working after switching loadouts.

Fixed bugs with several Pyreblaster perks affecting Pyreblaster Canister equipment.

Fixed several issues with Servo Skull objective in Siege Mode.

Fixed lots of issues with Ordeals.

The ordeal "Preserve Ammunition" now counts incapacitating shots as well as kills.

Fixed several issues with host migration.

Fixed bug where Pyreblaster Canister were not spawning when purchasing grenades in the Requisition Terminal in Siege.

Fixed lots of minor issues with Blessing and Atonement in Siege mode.

Fixed minor geometry issues on Purgation.

Minor customisation fixes.

Minor localisation fixes.

Minor UI fixes.
item.content:encoded:
Hotfix 13.1

GAMEPLAY AND BALANCING TWEAKS

Firearms in PvE

Gunstrike damage values restored to pre 13.0 values.

Plasma pistol variants gun strike damage adjusted to better reflect new versions speciality (i.e. damage version deals more gunstrike damage).

Siege Mode

Atonements:

  • Players will start receiving atonements starting from wave 11 on normal difficulty.

  • Players will start receiving atonements starting from wave 6 on hard difficulty.

A new indicator has been added to alert you when a new blessing becomes available.

Additional Blessing: Surplus - You can carry twice as much Equipment. Equipment spawns are doubled.

Additional Atonement: Hardened Skins - Enemies are not staggered by the first 3 hits. Damage is still dealt.

Additional Atonement: Posthumous Proliferation - Slain enemies spawn Spores or Chaos Skulls (depending on faction).

Additional Atonement: Booby Trap - Majoris-level and higher enemies explode upon death and deal Damage to Squad Members.

Ordeals

Reduced the requirements for the 6 "complete missions on absolute difficulty" class-specific ordeals from 10/30/100 to 10/25/50.

GENERAL FIXES

Crash fixes.

Consoles: Crashes when the French and Spanish VO were selected have been fixed.

Fixed a bug where several perks would reset their cooldown after switching loadouts.

Fixed a bug where several perks would stop working after switching loadouts.

Fixed bugs with several Pyreblaster perks affecting Pyreblaster Canister equipment.

Fixed several issues with Servo Skull objective in Siege Mode.

Fixed lots of issues with Ordeals.

The ordeal "Preserve Ammunition" now counts incapacitating shots as well as kills.

Fixed several issues with host migration.

Fixed bug where Pyreblaster Canister were not spawning when purchasing grenades in the Requisition Terminal in Siege.

Fixed lots of minor issues with Blessing and Atonement in Siege mode.

Fixed minor geometry issues on Purgation.

Minor customisation fixes.

Minor localisation fixes.

Minor UI fixes.

item.title: Flesh Is Weak — A First Look at the Augmentation System
item.description:
Do you aspire to the purity of the blessed machine, Lord Captain?

DLC3: The Infinite Museion for Warhammer 40,000: Rogue Trader is on its way, and the time has finally come to talk about its key feature - the augmentation system!

Available through the new voidship compartment — the Sector Bionica — the augmentation system is a brand new layer of character progression that allows you to install powerful cybernetic and bionic implants.

With over 100 augments scattered across the game, it will offer you a rich selection of new tactical possibilities.

So, let’s dive in!

The Sector Bionica





This place is a brand new medical and augmentation compartment on the von Valancius voidship that is available at any time to all owners of the Infinite Museion DLC.

Here, Genetor Ferrenc and Vivisector Martinax treat the sick and the wounded, perform surgeries, and compete for the attention of the Lord Captain. Each of them has their own approach and vision for their job, and each will gladly offer the Rogue Trader their advice, knowledge, and of course, augmentation services.




How Do the Augments Work?

Augments are a new special class of items that can be obtained across the Koronus Expanse and implanted into your characters. They typically have up to three unique effects:

  • A major positive effect that grants a significant advantage, changes standard gameplay mechanics, or allows the use of new abilities.
  • An impactful negative effect that works as a counterweight for the positive one and forces you to adapt your tactics around its disadvantage.
  • A tradeoff effect that grants a benefit in exchange for a negative effect. Some implants may lack this option.



There are six basic slots where the augments can be placed:

Head - 2 slots:

  • Perception system - augments that affect sight, sound, and smell or substitute for these, usually at the cost of one's own flesh.
  • Nervous system - cranial implants that affect reflexes, mind, and thought. These can be extremely dangerous for the user, yet many are willing to take the risks for an immediate tactical advantage.
Torso - 1 slot:

  • Internal organs - replacement organ augments that provide a variety of global body effects.
Hands - 2 slots:

  • Main hand - augmented main limb that may serve as a specialized tool or weapon.
  • Offhand - augmented off-hand, focusing on secondary and utility functions.
Legs - 1 slot:

  • Leg replacements - replacement augments for lower limbs that offer a number of mobility options.



Adeptus Mechanicus and Forgeworld characters have additional slots unique to their backgrounds and physiology.

Becoming Augmented

All augments can be installed through a painful surgery process in Sector Bionica after you obtain them as items. Once implanted, an augment cannot be removed completely - only replaced with a different one.

Note that not all of your companions will agree to this process - Astartes advanced biology requires a different approach not achievable on a Rogue Trader voidship, the Aeldari and Drukhari despise these modifications, and Cassia will simply never allow herself to be touched.

Others, however, will be happy to embrace the Machine God’s blessing.



Many implants are Conviction-locked or have effects based on what Conviction the user belongs to. That includes your companions: Argenta, for example, will never agree to wear anything Heretical.

All existing companions who, prior to this update, had augmented body parts in their appearance or story, such as Abelard, Jae, Solomorne and Pasqal, will also receive them as unique story augments.
Notably, these will remain available and provide effects even if the player does not own the Infinite Museion DLC.



Some implants may also be purely cosmetic, such as those selected during character generation.

Ferrum Sanctum

By inserting augments, you boost the character’s Ferrum Sanctum, representing their steps on the path to the purity of the blessed machine.

Each point of Ferrum Sanctum makes the character more susceptible to electricity and EMP damage, but boosts the effectiveness of some augments, talents, and abilities that synergize with this stat or target mechanical units.

Galvanization

Galvanization is a special overdrive mechanic available to certain augments.

When out of combat, you may place one of your augments with this feature in a special galvanization slot. In combat, you may choose to activate it, granting you a major benefit for one round in exchange for penalties in the following round.



Are you willing to take the risk, Lord Captain?

New Talents Related to Augments

As always with the new options, we’re adding a handful of new talents to support the new mechanics.

Here are just a few:







These provide you additional tactical flexibility and allow you to synergize the rest of your build with the new augmentation system.

What else?

We know you’re dying to find out more about the specific augments available, so here’s a couple more examples to give you an idea of their capabilities:





Are you planning which parts of your crude biomass you will replace yet?
Tell us in the comments.

Praise the Omnissiah!
item.content:encoded:
Do you aspire to the purity of the blessed machine, Lord Captain?

DLC3: The Infinite Museion for Warhammer 40,000: Rogue Trader is on its way, and the time has finally come to talk about its key feature - the augmentation system!

Available through the new voidship compartment — the Sector Bionica — the augmentation system is a brand new layer of character progression that allows you to install powerful cybernetic and bionic implants.

With over 100 augments scattered across the game, it will offer you a rich selection of new tactical possibilities.

So, let’s dive in!

The Sector Bionica





This place is a brand new medical and augmentation compartment on the von Valancius voidship that is available at any time to all owners of the Infinite Museion DLC.

Here, Genetor Ferrenc and Vivisector Martinax treat the sick and the wounded, perform surgeries, and compete for the attention of the Lord Captain. Each of them has their own approach and vision for their job, and each will gladly offer the Rogue Trader their advice, knowledge, and of course, augmentation services.




How Do the Augments Work?

Augments are a new special class of items that can be obtained across the Koronus Expanse and implanted into your characters. They typically have up to three unique effects:

  • A major positive effect that grants a significant advantage, changes standard gameplay mechanics, or allows the use of new abilities.
  • An impactful negative effect that works as a counterweight for the positive one and forces you to adapt your tactics around its disadvantage.
  • A tradeoff effect that grants a benefit in exchange for a negative effect. Some implants may lack this option.



There are six basic slots where the augments can be placed:

Head - 2 slots:

  • Perception system - augments that affect sight, sound, and smell or substitute for these, usually at the cost of one's own flesh.
  • Nervous system - cranial implants that affect reflexes, mind, and thought. These can be extremely dangerous for the user, yet many are willing to take the risks for an immediate tactical advantage.
Torso - 1 slot:

  • Internal organs - replacement organ augments that provide a variety of global body effects.
Hands - 2 slots:

  • Main hand - augmented main limb that may serve as a specialized tool or weapon.
  • Offhand - augmented off-hand, focusing on secondary and utility functions.
Legs - 1 slot:

  • Leg replacements - replacement augments for lower limbs that offer a number of mobility options.



Adeptus Mechanicus and Forgeworld characters have additional slots unique to their backgrounds and physiology.

Becoming Augmented

All augments can be installed through a painful surgery process in Sector Bionica after you obtain them as items. Once implanted, an augment cannot be removed completely - only replaced with a different one.

Note that not all of your companions will agree to this process - Astartes advanced biology requires a different approach not achievable on a Rogue Trader voidship, the Aeldari and Drukhari despise these modifications, and Cassia will simply never allow herself to be touched.

Others, however, will be happy to embrace the Machine God’s blessing.



Many implants are Conviction-locked or have effects based on what Conviction the user belongs to. That includes your companions: Argenta, for example, will never agree to wear anything Heretical.

All existing companions who, prior to this update, had augmented body parts in their appearance or story, such as Abelard, Jae, Solomorne and Pasqal, will also receive them as unique story augments.
Notably, these will remain available and provide effects even if the player does not own the Infinite Museion DLC.



Some implants may also be purely cosmetic, such as those selected during character generation.

Ferrum Sanctum

By inserting augments, you boost the character’s Ferrum Sanctum, representing their steps on the path to the purity of the blessed machine.

Each point of Ferrum Sanctum makes the character more susceptible to electricity and EMP damage, but boosts the effectiveness of some augments, talents, and abilities that synergize with this stat or target mechanical units.

Galvanization

Galvanization is a special overdrive mechanic available to certain augments.

When out of combat, you may place one of your augments with this feature in a special galvanization slot. In combat, you may choose to activate it, granting you a major benefit for one round in exchange for penalties in the following round.



Are you willing to take the risk, Lord Captain?

New Talents Related to Augments

As always with the new options, we’re adding a handful of new talents to support the new mechanics.

Here are just a few:







These provide you additional tactical flexibility and allow you to synergize the rest of your build with the new augmentation system.

What else?

We know you’re dying to find out more about the specific augments available, so here’s a couple more examples to give you an idea of their capabilities:





Are you planning which parts of your crude biomass you will replace yet?
Tell us in the comments.

Praise the Omnissiah!

item.title: Hotfix 6.11.1 - 25th of May
item.description:
Hotfix 6.11.1 - 25th of May
Features & Tweaks

  • Elven Axe:

    • Increased Push Attack damage to the first target.

    • Increased minimum targets before minimum damage on Push attack by 1.

  • Increased the damage of Warpick Heavy attacks (charged and uncharged) on critical hits against Super Armoured enemies.

Fixes

  • Fixed a crash on Steam authentication failure and implemented a feature to retry upon failure.

    • This should help with some connection issues.

  • Reverted unintended ammo buff to Griffonfoot.

  • Fixed a bug that caused War Pick heavies (both charged and uncharged) to deal lower damage to Armoured enemies on critical hits.

  • Fixed a crash when a Warrior Priest of Sigmar gets punched 10 times by a Chaos Warrior in the same game. No, really.

  • Fixed a bug causing the 1H Axe & Elven Axe not being able to proc "On Charged Attack Hit" talents.

  • Fixed a stuck spot in Skittergate, added failsafes for invincibility during the actual Skittergate section.

Dev note: We have seen your concerns regarding the changes to the Longbow. We intend to adress this in a future patch, but we need to do some testing before committing to a solution.
item.content:encoded:
Hotfix 6.11.1 - 25th of May
Features & Tweaks

  • Elven Axe:

    • Increased Push Attack damage to the first target.

    • Increased minimum targets before minimum damage on Push attack by 1.

  • Increased the damage of Warpick Heavy attacks (charged and uncharged) on critical hits against Super Armoured enemies.

Fixes

  • Fixed a crash on Steam authentication failure and implemented a feature to retry upon failure.

    • This should help with some connection issues.

  • Reverted unintended ammo buff to Griffonfoot.

  • Fixed a bug that caused War Pick heavies (both charged and uncharged) to deal lower damage to Armoured enemies on critical hits.

  • Fixed a crash when a Warrior Priest of Sigmar gets punched 10 times by a Chaos Warrior in the same game. No, really.

  • Fixed a bug causing the 1H Axe & Elven Axe not being able to proc "On Charged Attack Hit" talents.

  • Fixed a stuck spot in Skittergate, added failsafes for invincibility during the actual Skittergate section.

Dev note: We have seen your concerns regarding the changes to the Longbow. We intend to adress this in a future patch, but we need to do some testing before committing to a solution.
